description: Fix inaccuracies in MCP server reference, troubleshooting, and CLI reference documentation pages — wrong browser tool names, incorrect regex, incomplete shorthand expansion description, and missing WebSocket methods.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix browser tool names in /health example and add missing WebSocket methods
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want the MCP server reference /health example to show the actual browser tool names and the WebSocket protocol table to list all methods so that I can use the API correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: The `/health` response example in `docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx` uses actual browser tool names (e.g., `browser_navigate_tab`, `browser_click_element`, `browser_screenshot_tab`) instead of shortened forms (`browser_navigate`, `browser_click`, `browser_screenshot`)

The Extension → Server WebSocket table includes rows for `config.setToolsEnabled` (Request — batch enable/disable multiple tools), `config.setBrowserToolEnabled` (Request — enable/disable a single browser tool), and `config.setAllBrowserToolsEnabled` (Request — enable/disable all browser tools at once)
userStories.notes:
File: `docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx`

**Issue 1 (line 167):** The `browserToolNames` array in the example `/health` response uses shortened names:
```json
"browserToolNames": ["browser_navigate", "browser_click", "browser_screenshot"]
```
Change to actual tool names:
```json
"browserToolNames": ["browser_navigate_tab", "browser_click_element", "browser_screenshot_tab"]
```
The actual names are confirmed in `platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/navigate-tab.ts` (line 11: `name: 'browser_navigate_tab'`), `click-element.ts` (line 10: `name: 'browser_click_element'`), and `screenshot-tab.ts` (line 10: `name: 'browser_screenshot_tab'`).

**Issue 2 (around lines 433-518):** The Extension → Server WebSocket table is missing three methods that exist in `platform/mcp-server/src/extension-protocol.ts` lines 428-441:
- `config.setToolsEnabled` (line 428) — batch enable/disable multiple tools at once
- `config.setBrowserToolEnabled` (line 433) — enable/disable a single browser tool
- `config.setAllBrowserToolsEnabled` (line 438) — enable/disable all browser tools at once

Add three rows to the table following the same format as existing `config.*` entries. All three are Request type, used by the side panel UI.

userStories.title: Fix plugin name validation regex in troubleshooting page
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the troubleshooting page to show the correct plugin name validation regex so that I understand which names are actually valid.
acceptanceCriteria: The regex shown in `docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx` is `^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$` (matching the actual `NAME_REGEX` in `platform/shared/src/index.ts` line 424)

The description explains the pattern correctly: lowercase alphanumeric segments separated by single hyphens — no consecutive hyphens, no leading/trailing hyphens, digits allowed as the first character
userStories.notes:
File: `docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx` (around line 176)

Current text says:
> Names must match `^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` and not be reserved (e.g., `browser`, `extension`).

This is wrong in three ways:
1. The actual regex is `^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$` (from `platform/shared/src/index.ts` line 424)
2. The doc regex requires first char to be a letter; actual allows digits too
3. The doc regex allows consecutive hyphens (`my--plugin`) and trailing hyphens (`plugin-`); actual forbids both

Change to:
> Names must match `^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$` (lowercase alphanumeric segments separated by single hyphens) and not be reserved (e.g., `browser`, `extension`).

userStories.title: Fix plugin shorthand expansion description in CLI reference
userStories.description: As a user, I want the CLI reference to accurately describe how plugin shorthand names are resolved so that I understand the resolution order.
acceptanceCriteria: The `opentabs plugin install` section in `docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx` explains that shorthand names check the official scoped package first (`@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-<name>`) then fall back to the community unscoped package (`opentabs-plugin-<name>`)
The examples still show the simple shorthand usage (`opentabs plugin install slack`) but the description accurately reflects the two-candidate resolution
userStories.notes:
File: `docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx` (around line 513)

Current text:
> Shorthand names are automatically expanded: `slack` becomes `opentabs-plugin-slack`.

This is incomplete. The actual behavior in `platform/shared/src/constants.ts` `resolvePluginPackageCandidates()` (lines 77-81) returns TWO candidates for shorthand names: `['@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack', 'opentabs-plugin-slack']`. The install handler in `platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts` lines 104-111 checks both concurrently via npm and picks the first match by priority (official scoped first, then community unscoped).

Change to something like:
> Shorthand names are resolved by checking the official scoped package first (`@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack`), then falling back to the community unscoped package (`opentabs-plugin-slack`). Runs `npm install -g` under the hood. If the MCP server is running, it is notified via `POST /reload` to pick up the new plugin immediately.
